durable_residue.learned constraint: Keep the first checked-in aid manifest format JSON because current structured inventory proof executes JSON Schema for JSON files.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: Manifest metadata prevents checked-in aids from becoming unowned scripts while keeping ordinary startup unchanged.
durable_residue.promotion trigger: when aid manifest shape, aid storage, or candidate-aid validation changes

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #518 was the manifest/schema slice; portability/safety depth, discovery, and promotion remain planned children.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: schema, checker, structured inventory, compact defaults payload, and docs.
closure_check.reopen trigger: checked-in aid files can bypass manifest metadata, or manifest metadata cannot express required safety, validation, promotion, and retirement fields.

signals found: Subagent inspection shortened the manifest-format decision and prevented a TOML/YAML schema claim the current checker cannot execute.
The proof selector correctly surfaced direct CLI/defaults edits as needing root CLI authority review.
signals fixed: Adopted `manifest.json` and kept TOML/YAML support out of this slice.
Ran root CLI authority proof instead of treating the defaults edit as ordinary contract-only work.
